content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

November 10, 1976

Honorable J. P. Vukasin, Jr.
Judge, Superior Court
State of California
County Courthouse/Alameda
Oakland, California 94612

Dear Judge Vukasin:

On October 29 I wrote you concerning the question of the necessity of reporting, on a "Statement of Economic Interests" filed pursuant to Government Code §87200, et seq., certain types of bonds issued by local governmental agencies. In that letter I also indicated that a filer would only report investments and interests in real property on his initial statement filed.

The latter statement is not correct in the case of judges of courts of record. AB 809, which extended the coverage of Government Code §87200 to judges, does not provide for filing an "initial statement". Rather, the first statement filed, which will be early in 1977, will in essence be an anniversary statement (see Government Code §87203), listing investments, interests in real property, and income held during the period covered by the statement. In this case, that period will be May 5, 1976, to December 31, 1976. A copy of the bill is enclosed.

In light of what we concluded concerning the specific investments you asked about, this is probably an academic matter; but I wanted to correct any false impressions I may have given you.

Sincerely,

Kenneth W. Goshorn
Research Specialist
Conflicts of Interest
Division

KWG:glb
enc.

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95604 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

October 29, 1976

Honorable J. P. Vukasin, Jr.
Judge, Superior Court
State of California
County Alameda Courthouse
Oakland, California 94612

Dear Judge Vukasin:

I have been asked to respond to your letter to the Commission
dated October 14.

First, let me point out that since the Political Reform Act
has only recently been amended to provide that judges of courts
of record file statements of economic interests pursuant to
Government Code Section 87200, in filing your initial statement,
you should only report "investments" and "interests in real property"
(see Section 87202). Only upon filing your first anniversary state-
ment will you be required to report income during the period
covered (see Section 87203).

Government Code Sections 82034 and 82030 exclude from the
definitions of "investment" and "income" any "bond or other debt
instrument issued by ...government or government agency", or income
derived therefrom. "Agency" means any "state agency" or "local
government agency" (Section 82003). Local government agency, as
defined in Section 82041, means "a county, city, or district of any
kind, including school districts, or any other local or regional
political subdivision, or any department, division, bureau, office,
board, commission, or other agency of these..." It certainly appears
to us that all the investments cited in your letter fit within this
definition and, thus, are not the subject of any reporting obliga-
tion under the statute.

With respect to your second question, the answer is "yes".
Concededly, this is an anomalous result in light of the exclusion
of similar monies received from state and local government agency
sources, see Section 82030(b)(2). The desirability of amending
the statute to resolve this inconsistency can certainly be argued,
but I must, given the present state of the law, advise you that if
such income is in excess of $250 during the period, it should be
reported pursuant to Section 87207.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (SIA-222-6440 • EXECUTIVE (SCA-222-5681)

Honorable J. P. Vukasin, Jr.        -2-        October 29, 1976


     Thank you for writing. If I can be of further assistance in
this or any other matter, please do not hesitate to call on me at
(916) 322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mra

Superior Court
State of California

J.P. VUKASIN, JR.
JUDGE

COUNTY OF ALAMEDA
COURT HOUSE-OAKLAND 94612

October 14, 1976

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

For purposes of preparing Statement of Economic Interests,
Form 721, please provide the following information:

INVESTMENTS OVER $1000

Do the following bonds fall in the category of exceptions
identified as bonds or other debt instruments issued by any
government agency:

California State College, Los Angeles, 5.4 percent
Student Union Revenue Bond, principal due March 1, 1992;
Board of Port Commissioners for the City of Oakland,
7.5 percent, Port of Oakland 1957 Revenue Bond,
Series M, principal due August 1, 1977;
Department of Water and Power of the City of Los Angeles,
Water Works Revenue Bond, Second issue of 1975,
principal due November 15, 2015;
California Educational Facilities Authority, University
of Southern California Project Revenue Bond, Series A
principal due October 15, 2000;
Glendale Redevelopment Agency, Central Glendale Redevelop-
ment Project Tax Allocation Bond, Series A, principal
due May 1, 1992.

INCOME AGGREGATING $250 OR MORE

Is the interest income from the above listed bonds interest
from a "Debt Instrument Issued by a Government or Government
Agency" so as to fall within the exception to the listing of
income aggregating $250 or more?

-1-

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
October 14, 1976


Is it necessary to list reimbursement received from the
federal government for actual travel expenses incurred?

Thank you for your cooperation.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

JUDGE J. P. VUKASIN, JR.


JPV:la


-2-

sections.question: Whether certain types of bonds issued by local governmental agencies must be reported on a Statement of Economic Interests filed pursuant to Government Code §87200, et seq., and whether judges must report investments and interests in real property on their initial statement.
sections.conclusion: Government Code Sections 82034 and 82030 exclude from the definitions of 'investment' and 'income' any 'bond or other debt obligation' issued by government or government agency. Local government agencies are defined broadly in Section 82041 to include counties, cities, districts, and other regional agencies. All investments cited in Judge Vukasin's letter fit within this exclusion and are not subject to any reporting obligation under the statute. Additionally, the first statement filed by judges in 1977 will be an anniversary statement (not an 'initial statement') covering the period from May 5, 1976, to December 31, 1976, and will require reporting of investments, interests in real property, and income held during that period.
sections.question_synthetic: Judge Vukasin asked whether bonds issued by local governmental agencies must be reported on Statements of Economic Interests under Government Code §87200, and what types of investments judges must initially report.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that bonds issued by local governmental agencies are excluded from reporting requirements under Government Code §82030 and §82034, and that Judge Vukasin's specific investments are not reportable. The FPPC also clarified that judges' first filing will be an anniversary statement (not an initial statement) covering May 5, 1976, through December 31, 1976, requiring full reporting of investments, real property interests, and income for that period.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two letters: October 29, 1976 advice letter with formal Q/A structure, and November 10, 1976 follow-up correction letter. The question and conclusion sections are embedded in the narrative format rather than being clearly labeled with headers. Document quality is poor with OCR errors and missing text sections, but core advice is extractable. The November letter corrects a statement from the October letter regarding initial vs. anniversary statements for judges.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether certain types of bonds issued by local governmental agencies must be reported on a Statement of Economic Interests filed pursuant to Government Code §87200, et seq., and whether judges must report investments and interests in real property on their initial statement.

CONCLUSION: Government Code Sections 82034 and 82030 exclude from the definitions of 'investment' and 'income' any 'bond or other debt obligation' issued by government or government agency. Local government agencies are defined broadly in Section 82041 to include counties, cities, districts, and other regional agencies. All investments cited in Judge Vukasin's letter fit within this exclusion and are not subject to any reporting obligation under the statute. Additionally, the first statement filed by judges in 1977 will be an anniversary statement (not an 'initial statement') covering the period from May 5, 1976, to December 31, 1976, and will require reporting of investments, interests in real property, and income held during that period.

embedding.summary: This is a two-part FPPC response letter from October 29, 1976 (with a follow-up correction from November 10, 1976) to Judge J. P. Vukasin, Jr., clarifying that bonds issued by local governmental agencies are exempt from reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act, and correcting prior advice about the nature of judges' initial Statement of Economic Interests filings.
